subject: cotal.console-r5.inst.601ea22f-6c66-4b93-afab-5242fc373ce5.0abe37de-6fde-42f5-a590-241b7e57c33b
text:
You're research on the console-ink build. Your task:

1. READ `examples/04-self-improving-console/research/INPUT.md` (that's the real path; the SPEC placeholder points there too).
2. VERIFY the key facts against the actual code — especially `implementations/cli/src/commands/console.ts` (the read-only CotalEndpoint observer we render over) and `@cotal/core` (what the endpoint exposes: presence/roster, channel taps, message events, history). Don't take INPUT.md on faith; confirm method/event names exist.
3. WRITE the SPEC to `implementations/cli/src/console/SPEC.md` — overwrite the placeholder. Cover: the observer contract we build on (NO new NATS connection), the data the UI needs (roster, channel tabs, live feed, presence/status), and the target UX (roster panel + channel tabs + live message feed + multi-panel focus + context-sensitive `?` help overlay). Keep it tight and concrete — backend and tui-designer build directly against it.
4. cotal_send a short summary to the `team` channel so backend and tui-designer get the right context.

Reply to me with `done:` when the SPEC is written and broadcast. Ask me if blocked.

subject: cotal.console-r5.chat.601ea22f-6c66-4b93-afab-5242fc373ce5.team
text:
SPEC ready → implementations/cli/src/console/SPEC.md. Build a lazygit-style Ink TUI over the EXISTING read-only CotalEndpoint observer (commands/console.ts) — NO new NATS connection. It's a port of render.ts's runDashboard.

Observer API (all verified in @cotal/core): ep.getRoster()/on("roster") for the roster snapshot; ep.tap((subject,msg?)=>…,{subject?}) for the live feed (use deliveryOf(subject) to classify multicast/unicast/anycast; null=drop); ep.listChannels() for tabs; ep.channelHistory(ch,{limit}) for backlog. NO "message" event — observer has consume:false, feed comes only from tap. Unicast `to` is an instance id → resolve via roster byId map. Reuse render.ts logic: RosterIndex (status-sort + byId), Fanout (400ms burst coalescer), 300-entry window, agentColor.

UX: roster panel (left) · channel tabs (top, 1–9 jump) · live feed (main, pinned-to-bottom) · useFocus multi-panel · context-sensitive `?` overlay · status bar (msgs/s).

useMesh() shape in SPEC is a PROPOSAL — @backend (mesh.ts) + @tui-designer (app.tsx/ui) finalize it together; that interface is your shared contract. Open Qs flagged in the SPEC. Ping me with questions.

subject: cotal.console-r5.inst.49210627-7452-4436-8646-c85cf94e071f.0abe37de-6fde-42f5-a590-241b7e57c33b
text:
You're backend on the console-ink build. Your task:

BUILD `implementations/cli/src/console/mesh.ts` — a `useMesh()` React hook (Ink) over the EXISTING read-only CotalEndpoint observer (see `implementations/cli/src/commands/console.ts` — the same observer config: consume:false, registerPresence:false, watchPresence:true). DO NOT open a new raw NATS connection; render over the passed-in endpoint. The hook returns UI-ready state: roster (with presence/status/activity), channel list/tabs, live message feed, and whatever the UI needs.

CONTEXT: research wrote `implementations/cli/src/console/SPEC.md` and broadcast a summary to #team (in your inbox). Read both first — research verified the observer's real surface (getRoster, presence events, tap, listChannels, channelHistory; note NO "message" event since consume:false).

CRITICAL: settle the EXACT `useMesh()` interface DIRECTLY with `tui-designer` over cotal_dm — agree on the shape together (return type, field names, how the feed is fed). Do NOT route the contract through me; talk to tui-designer peer-to-peer. research can answer endpoint-surface questions.

Reply `done:` to me when mesh.ts is built and `pnpm --filter @cotal/cli typecheck` is green. Ask me only if blocked on a non-technical decision.

subject: cotal.console-r5.inst.b2c814aa-9906-41f0-b8df-8fd407544171.0abe37de-6fde-42f5-a590-241b7e57c33b
text:
You're tui-designer on the console-ink build. Your task:

BUILD the Ink components in `implementations/cli/src/console/` — `app.tsx` + `ui/*.tsx`. Target a polished lazygit-style TUI: roster panel + channel tabs + live message feed + multi-panel focus (tab/arrow to switch panels) + a context-sensitive `?` help overlay. WIRE them into the `console-ink` command (scaffold at `implementations/cli/src/commands/console-ink.tsx`), which must construct the SAME read-only observer as `commands/console.ts` (consume:false, registerPresence:false, watchPresence:true) and render the Ink app over it — NO new raw NATS connection.

CONTEXT: research wrote `implementations/cli/src/console/SPEC.md` and broadcast a summary to #team (in your inbox). Read both first.

CRITICAL: settle the EXACT `useMesh()` interface DIRECTLY with `backend` over cotal_dm — backend owns `mesh.ts`; agree together on the return shape your components consume. Do NOT route the contract through me; talk to backend peer-to-peer.

Reply `done:` to me when the components are built, the command is wired, and `pnpm --filter @cotal/cli typecheck` is green. Ask me only if blocked on a non-technical decision.

subject: cotal.console-r5.inst.effb2587-6115-4ff9-84ef-e2f3d871eb22.0abe37de-6fde-42f5-a590-241b7e57c33b
text:
You're the critical reviewer — a sharp second pair of eyes. Right now: review the PLAN, not code yet.

Read `implementations/cli/src/console/SPEC.md` (research also broadcast a summary to #team). Pressure-test it against the goal: a lazygit-style Ink/React TUI shipped as `cotal console-ink`, rendering over the EXISTING read-only CotalEndpoint observer (`implementations/cli/src/commands/console.ts`) — NO new NATS connection. Does the SPEC (a) correctly build on that observer's real surface, (b) cover roster panel + channel tabs + live feed + multi-panel focus + `?` help overlay, and (c) propose a sound `useMesh()` shape the backend/tui-designer can finalize? Call out gaps, risks, and anything underspecified.

Post your findings to the #team channel (cotal_send channel="team"). This is non-blocking — I'll relay actionable items to the right author. A code review pass comes later once the workers finish.

subject: cotal.console-r5.inst.49210627-7452-4436-8646-c85cf94e071f.b2c814aa-9906-41f0-b8df-8fd407544171
cotal.console-r5.inst.b2c814aa-9906-41f0-b8df-8fd407544171.49210627-7452-4436-8646-c85cf94e071f
cotal.console-r5.inst.b2c814aa-9906-41f0-b8df-8fd407544171.49210627-7452-4436-8646-c85cf94e071f
text:
Agreed — full feed, no in-hook channel filter. Locking exports: useMesh, MeshState, FeedEntry, signature useMesh(ep, opts?: { window?; tapSubject? }). One thing to nail down since I can't edit console-ink.tsx:

The HOOK owns the endpoint lifecycle. useMesh does, in a useEffect: attach error listener → await ep.start() → ep.tap(cb) → feed internal store; cleanup calls ep.stop(). So your console-ink command should just CREATE the observer ep (unstarted, the console.ts config) and render(<App ep={ep} tapSubject={creds?chatWildcard(space):undefined}/>) — do NOT start()/tap() there, the hook does it. status.connected flips true after start() resolves.

That's why I added tapSubject? to opts: under auth the tap must narrow to chatWildcard(space); open-mode demo = undefined. Just thread it App→useMesh. If you'd rather not, I default to whole-space tap (breaks under creds) — but threading it is one prop. OK with hook-owns-lifecycle + tapSubject opt? If yes I'll push mesh.ts now.

subject: cotal.console-r5.chat.effb2587-6115-4ff9-84ef-e2f3d871eb22.team
text:
review: console-ink SPEC — solid & well-grounded (every endpoint method/event/type it names is real; "no message event on the observer" is correct — emit("message") only fires from pump(), which consume:false never starts). console-ink.tsx is a REAL Ink app on the real observer (mirrors console.ts), not a stub command — but app.tsx + mesh.ts are still export{} TODOs. 7 gaps to close before building:

1. AUTH MODE KILLS UNICAST+ANYCAST. Under --creds the tap is narrowed to chat.> (DMs/anycast stay confidential). So Fanout's whole unicast-coalescing path, byId/nameOf resolution, and FeedEntry.toNames/count/toService only ever fire in open mode. SPEC copies the narrowing line but never states the consequence: auth-mode feed is channels-only. UI must SIGNAL "DMs hidden under auth", not silently show a thin feed.

2. CHANNEL TABS HAVE NO HOME FOR UNICAST/ANYCAST. Feed is "filtered by active channel," but unicast(to)/anycast(toService) carry no channel → with tabs, peer DMs + anycast (a core "all 3 addressing modes" demo point) vanish. Need an explicit "all"/firehose tab (+ maybe a DMs/@svc pseudo-tab).

3. TAB SET = listChannels() ∪ channels-seen-in-feed. listChannels() is a periodic JS snapshot; a peer opening a NEW channel strands its messages until the next refresh. Derive tabs from the union.

4. COALESCE vs ID-DEDUP CONFLICT. SPEC lists "dedupe history/live by id" as open — but Fanout merges N msgs into ONE entry, so FeedEntry.id is ambiguous. Also: order on msg.ts, NOT receive wall-clock (render.ts's time is display-only) — else channelHistory prefill interleaves wrong. Define the coalesced-entry id + ts source.

5. SCROLL+MOUSE IS THE HARD PART, hand-waved. Ink has NO native scroll viewport; render.ts does it by hand w/ raw ANSI + xterm alt-scroll (\x1b[?1007h → arrows). In Ink you must slice feed to rows (useStdout) + track pinnedToBottom yourself, and MOUSE WHEEL is NOT delivered to useInput by default (needs manual mouse-mode + escape parsing). PgUp/PgDn/Home/End are fine; budget mouse wheel as a real risk / likely-custom.

6. HELP OVERLAY MUST SWALLOW INPUT. Ink useInput handlers all fire regardless of focus unless gated. A "modal that takes focus" needs the panel handlers gated (isActive) or 1–9/Tab/q still fire underneath.

7. TIMER LIFECYCLE. Fanout's 400ms flush + ~50-100ms render ticker + 1s age refresh + listChannels refresh all mutate state outside React → useMesh must clean ALL up on unmount (render.ts calls fan.stop()) + batch into one setState, or you leak timers / setState-after-unmount.

REUSE CAVEAT: reuse render.ts Fanout/RosterIndex LOGIC, but NOT LogEntry — its head/body are pre-baked ANSI (c.cyan/agentColor). Ink wants <Text color>; agentColor returns an ANSI fn — extract the palette index instead. FeedEntry (structured) is the right call. useMesh() shape is sound; leaving hook-vs-store/filter-location/batching to backend↔tui-designer is correct. DMing specifics to both.

subject: cotal.console-r5.inst.49210627-7452-4436-8646-c85cf94e071f.effb2587-6115-4ff9-84ef-e2f3d871eb22
text:
mesh.ts / useMesh() specifics (you own the data layer):

• getRoster() AND the "roster" event sort by NAME ONLY (endpoint.ts:393-396). MeshState.agents is "status-sorted" → re-apply STATUS_ORDER in the hook. Just reuse render.ts RosterIndex (it already does status-sort + builds byId) — don't assume the snapshot arrives status-sorted.

• Auth split is yours to surface: under creds the tap subject is chat.> only → feed is multicast-only; unicast/anycast never arrive. Add something like status.dmVisible:boolean (false when creds set) so the UI can label it. Don't let the unicast coalescing path look "broken" when it's just confidential.

• FeedEntry.id under coalescing: Fanout merges N unicast msgs → one entry. Pick a deterministic id (first msg.id) and set ts = min(msg.ts) of the group, sourced from msg.ts NOT now(). This is what makes channelHistory(prefill) ∪ live tap dedupe-by-id actually work and order correctly.

• channelHistory prefill: only meaningful for a multicast tab (it's chat.*.<channel>). dmHistory() throws for a non-admin cred (ACL) — don't call it. Dedupe prefill vs live by id; live tap will re-deliver backlog-adjacent msgs.

• Tabs = listChannels() ∪ {channels seen live}. Pure listChannels() strands brand-new channels until the next poll.

• Lifecycle: funnel Fanout's 400ms flush + your ~50-100ms ticker + 1s age refresh + listChannels poll into ONE batched setState, and clean them ALL up on unmount (render.ts calls fan.stop()) — Fanout holds live setTimeouts.

• Reuse caveat: take Fanout/RosterIndex LOGIC but emit FeedEntry (structured), not LogEntry — LogEntry.head/body are pre-baked ANSI. agentColor() returns an ANSI wrapper; expose the palette index (name→PALETTE[h%len]) so tui-designer can map to <Text color>.

SPEC verified against endpoint.ts — every surface you need (getRoster/on(roster|presence|error)/tap/listChannels/channelHistory/start/stop) exists as documented. Good to build.

subject: cotal.console-r5.inst.b2c814aa-9906-41f0-b8df-8fd407544171.effb2587-6115-4ff9-84ef-e2f3d871eb22
text:
app.tsx + ui/* specifics (you own the UI):

• SCROLL is the hard part. Ink has NO scroll viewport. render.ts does it by hand: slice the feed to terminal rows (useStdout columns/rows) + track pinnedToBottom yourself; auto-follow tail unless scrolled up. MOUSE WHEEL is NOT delivered to useInput by default — render.ts enables xterm alt-scroll (\x1b[?1007h) so the wheel emits arrow keys. In Ink you'd write raw mouse-mode + parse escapes outside Ink, fragile. Budget PgUp/PgDn/Home/End (easy via useInput) as the baseline; treat mouse wheel as optional/custom, don't assume free.

• CHANNEL TABS need an "all"/firehose tab — unicast/anycast carry no channel, so a channel-filtered feed hides DMs + anycast (a core demo point). Add #all (+ optionally a DMs / @svc view). Tab set comes from useMesh (listChannels ∪ seen-live); 1–9 jump, Tab cycles.

• ? HELP OVERLAY must truly swallow input: Ink useInput handlers ALL fire regardless of focus unless gated. Gate every panel's useInput on an isActive flag the overlay flips, or 1–9/Tab/q fire under the modal. Single input-routing source.

• Don't render raw ANSI from render.ts. LogEntry.head/body are pre-colored ANSI strings — mixing them with Ink layout breaks measurement/wrap. Consume FeedEntry (structured fields) from useMesh and map to <Text color>. Ask backend to expose the per-agent palette index (not the ANSI agentColor fn).

• <Static> is append-only (no scroll) — only for finalized scrollback, keep the live tail in normal state (SPEC has this right). maxFps ~30 + incrementalRendering good.

• Stacked fallback <80 cols (SPEC notes it) — roster-left + feed becomes vertical.

FYI console-ink.tsx already renders a real <Placeholder/> wired to the live observer — swapping in your <App ep={ep}/> is a one-liner once app.tsx exists. Note the placeholder's tap(()=>setSeen++) counts EVERY frame incl. control/presence; your real feed must apply deliveryOf() (null = drop) like render.ts Fanout does.

subject: cotal.console-r5.inst.0abe37de-6fde-42f5-a590-241b7e57c33b.effb2587-6115-4ff9-84ef-e2f3d871eb22
text: done: reviewed the plan (SPEC.md), grounded against @cotal/core + console.ts/render.ts. Verdict: SPEC is solid and accurately grounded — every endpoint surface it names exists, and the "no message event on the observer" claim is correct. console-ink.tsx is already a REAL working Ink app on the real observer (not a stub command); app.tsx + mesh.ts are still export{} TODOs. Posted 7 actionable gaps to #team and DM'd specifics to backend (useMesh: name-vs-status sort, auth hides DMs, coalesced-entry id/ts, tab union, timer cleanup, reuse-logic-not-ANSI) and tui-designer (Ink has no scroll viewport + mouse wheel not free, need an "all"/firehose tab for unicast/anycast, modal must gate useInput, consume FeedEntry not ANSI). Top risk: scroll/mouse-wheel porting; biggest demo gap: channel tabs hide unicast+anycast. Available for the code-review pass once workers finish.

subject: cotal.console-r5.inst.49210627-7452-4436-8646-c85cf94e071f.b2c814aa-9906-41f0-b8df-8fd407544171
text:
YES to all three — lock it and push mesh.ts:

1) Single hook + internal store + ~75ms ticker batching ✓
2) Full feed, no in-hook channel filter ✓ (I add the #all firehose tab + per-channel filter in Feed)
3) Hook owns endpoint lifecycle (error listener → start() → tap(cb) → store; cleanup stop()) ✓ — so console-ink.tsx will ONLY create the unstarted observer ep and render(<App ep={ep} tapSubject={creds?chatWildcard(space):undefined}/>). I will NOT start/tap/stop in the command. Please make sure cleanup fully stops the ep so the command needs no stop() after waitUntilExit.

Exports I import from ./mesh.js: useMesh, MeshState, FeedEntry. Signature useMesh(ep, opts?: { window?: number; tapSubject?: string }). I'll call useMesh(ep, { tapSubject }).

On reviewer's palette-index idea: NOT needed — I compute per-agent color myself by hashing the name → hex (same hash everywhere, so sender + unicast toNames stay color-stable). So FeedEntry.text = plain parts-joined (no ansi), and from/toNames carry names; that's all I need. Don't add a palette field.

Building app.tsx + ui/* against exactly this now — typecheck goes green the moment your mesh.ts lands. 🚀

subject: cotal.console-r5.inst.effb2587-6115-4ff9-84ef-e2f3d871eb22.b2c814aa-9906-41f0-b8df-8fd407544171
text:
Thanks — all five landed in the design:
• Scroll: hand-rolled viewport (slice rows to useStdout rows, track scroll; auto-follow tail unless scrolled up). Keyboard baseline ↑↓/PgUp/PgDn/g·Home/G·End. Mouse wheel treated as optional, not assumed.
• Tabs: #all firehose tab (index 1) so unicast+anycast stay visible; per-channel tabs filter multicast only. 1–9 jump, Tab/←→ cycle panels.
• ? overlay: single input-routing source — global useInput owns 1-9/?/q/arrows; panel useInput gated on isActive=isFocused && !helpOpen; overlay swallows all keys (any key closes).
• No raw ANSI: consuming structured FeedEntry, mapping to <Text color>; I hash name→hex myself (told backend no palette field needed).
• <Static> intentionally NOT used for the live feed (it's a filterable scroll viewport); dynamic output kept ≤ viewport height + maxFps 30 + incrementalRendering.
Stacked fallback <80 cols too. Building now.

Landed. mesh.ts is on the branch, @cotal/cli typecheck green. Exports exactly: useMesh, MeshState, FeedEntry. Signature useMesh(ep, opts?: { window?; tapSubject? }) — your useMesh(ep, { tapSubject }) is correct.

Lifecycle: hook's useEffect cleanup calls store.stop() which clears all timers + pending burst setTimeouts AND awaits ep.stop() — so the command needs no stop() after waitUntilExit. ✓

One additive note: status also carries dmVisible:boolean (false when tapSubject set = auth, so unicast/anycast are confidential not missing). Purely additive to status — ignore it or use it to label the firehose. Go build — green the moment you import it. 🚀

subject: cotal.console-r5.inst.b2c814aa-9906-41f0-b8df-8fd407544171.effb2587-6115-4ff9-84ef-e2f3d871eb22
text:
Design looks right. Two things to watch:

1. ARROW ROUTING CONFLICT (real). You said global useInput owns ↑↓/PgUp/PgDn AND panels gate on isActive — but the feed's hand-rolled scroll NEEDS those keys when the feed panel is focused. If the global handler consumes arrows unconditionally, scroll never moves. Resolve it explicitly: either (a) global dispatches ↑↓/Pg/Home/End to the focused scrollable panel's scroll state, or (b) the feed panel owns arrows when isFocused and global only owns 1-9/?/q + Tab. Don't let both claim arrows. (← → for panel-cycle is fine — that's distinct from ↑↓ scroll.)

2. COLOR CONSISTENCY (minor, your call). Hashing name→hex yourself is clean, but it'll diverge from the classic `console` dashboard, which hashes name→256-color via PALETTE in render.ts (agentColor). Same agent → different color across the two consoles. Cosmetic only; if you want them to match, mirror render.ts's PALETTE indices, otherwise fine to ignore for the demo.

Everything else (#all firehose tab, modal swallow, no <Static> for live feed, stacked <80col) is exactly right. Ping me when it's wired to <App/> and I'll do the code-review pass.
